_meta.what_firm_does: Persona is a configurable identity verification platform for businesses—verifying individuals and organizations (e.g., Reddit for age assurance, OpenAI for abuse prevention). Sits on the Finance team for this role. HQ SF; this role is remote (US/Canada).
_meta.jd_wants: Design/build/maintain incentive (sales comp) pay models; own recurring comp review cycles (validate data integrity, calculate attainment, ensure accurate/on-time payouts); manage external comp consultant + timelines; go-to for pay/attainment questions; ad-hoc analysis. 3+ yrs finance/ops/accounting/analytical; high Excel/Sheets (complex models, messy data, scalable auditable systems); detail-oriented; discretion with sensitive comp data.
_meta.drafted_by: Opus 4.8 in Jamie's voice, mined from voice corpus + feedback rules
_meta.honest_gap_note: Significant stretch — this is a Finance/sales-comp analyst role, not People Ops. Jamie has strong data/Excel/audit + sensitive-data discretion, but no sales-comp-plan-design experience. Answers lead with transferable analytical rigor and are candid about the gap.
_meta.status: DRAFT — Jamie to review/edit before submit

Why are you interested in this role?: I'm drawn to roles where accurate data is the foundation everyone else relies on—and a comp-operations role is exactly that. I genuinely enjoy the work of owning a recurring cycle end-to-end: validating data integrity, catching the discrepancy before it becomes a problem, and making sure the right output goes out accurately and on time. At ODN I helped audit 300+ leave cases and built clean, executive-ready analysis on $362K in costs, and at InGenius I own recurring review cycles and build the spreadsheet models that track performance against plans. The high-trust, detail-obsessed nature of this role is squarely where I do my best work.
Tell us about your Excel / data modeling: Excel and Google Sheets are core tools for me—I build tracking models, work with messy data, and structure things so they're auditable and scalable. At InGenius I maintain the spreadsheet systems that monitor program performance and validate accuracy before deliverables go out; at NextGen I turned 2,000+ messy mixed-methods responses into clean, actionable reports; and at ODN I audit case-level data to refute cost assumptions with evidence. I'm the person who double-checks and doesn't let things slip—keeping data accurate and well-documented is genuinely something I take pride in.
Tell us about handling sensitive / confi: I've handled sensitive compensation and headcount data throughout my HR work—at Vestas across employee relations and succession planning, and at Kronos researching and modeling global compensation to inform offers. I'm careful, discreet, and calm when fielding questions about sensitive topics, and I understand that good judgment matters as much as accuracy. I'd bring that same discretion to comp data here.
Where are you less experienced, and how: I'll be candid—I haven't designed sales incentive-comp plans or owned a payout cycle in a Finance org specifically, so the GTM-comp domain would be new to me. But the underlying work—accurate modeling, validating data integrity, owning a recurring cycle, coordinating an external consultant and timelines, and handling sensitive data—is exactly what I already do. My approach to anything new is to diagnose before designing: I'd spend early time understanding the existing plans and data flows deeply (I'd rather over-invest in that upfront than move too fast without alignment), then build clean, auditable systems on top. I ramp fast and I'm genuinely curious about how the product and business work.
Why Persona?: I'm drawn to Persona because identity verification is one of those problems that quietly underpins trust across the whole internet—and doing it well in an AI-first world is genuinely hard and important. I also like that this role sits at the intersection of Finance and GTM, where the data directly shapes incentives and growth. My training in Applied Organizational Psychology makes me treat operations as a system to study and improve, and I'd be excited to bring that rigor to a high-trust analyst seat on your team.
